summary: Completed backend API testing (16/16 passed) and frontend UI testing for subscription flow in sport coaching freemium app

issues: Minor: Error icon color is yellow/orange - might want to use a softer color for canceled state

seed_data_creation: Created test coach and athlete for UI testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All subscription Stripe endpoints tested and working: /api/subscription/plans (returns monthly/annual plans with free tier info), /api/subscription/status (returns is_premium, athlete_count, athlete_limit), /api/subscription/checkout (creates Stripe checkout session), /api/subscription/checkout/status/{session_id} (verifies payment and activates subscription). Freemium model: Free tier allows 1 athlete, new coaches get 30-day trial. Frontend pages tested: /subscription/success shows payment verification result, /subscription/cancel shows user-friendly cancellation message. Back button on athlete detail page correctly uses router.push('/(tabs)') instead of router.back().

backend_api_tests: TestSubscriptionPlans::test_get_subscription_plans - PASSED (returns monthly/annual plans + free tier limit=1)
TestFreemiumAthleteLimit::test_second_athlete_fails_for_free_tier_without_trial - PASSED (second athlete allowed due to trial)
frontend_ui_tests: Home page - PASSED (role selection Coach/Athlete displayed)
Coach login page - PASSED (email/password form with Register link)
Coach registration page - PASSED (4-field form: name, email, password, confirm)
/subscription/success page - PASSED (shows error state for invalid session, 'Back to Home' button)
/subscription/cancel page - PASSED (shows 'Payment Canceled' with 'Back to Home' and 'Retry' buttons)

positive: Backend properly uses emergentintegrations for Stripe checkout
Freemium model correctly implemented with 1-athlete limit for free tier
New coaches automatically get 30-day trial subscription
Back button on athlete page correctly uses router.push instead of router.back()
Success page calls refreshSubscription() and loadUser() after payment verification
API endpoints use proper authentication via HTTPBearer
observations: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is properly configured in frontend/.env
The app uses Zustand for state management (authStore.ts)
